judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports independently reach the same correct root cause, timeline, disagreement with the flaky conclusion, and assessment of the model-swap plan, with comparable evidence quality; they are essentially tied in substance.

report_1.evidence: The fix belongs in **`ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml`**: change `expected_label_field: label` to `expected_label_field: gold_label` so it matches the eval set's actual key

report_2.evidence: The fix is a one-line change to `ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml`: change `expected_label_field: label` to `expected_label_field: gold_label`.

report_1.evidence: The regression began on **2026-06-17**, introduced by commit `53551e8` ("chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)", authored 2026-06-17 13:00 UTC)... `pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv` (via `git show e96cd06`) shows accuracy 0.90 (06-14), 0.92 (06-15), 0.91 (06-16), then 0.00 on 06-17, 06-18, 06-19 — the exact date of commit `53551e8`.

report_2.evidence: The regression began with commit `53551e8` — "chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)"... corroborated directly by the nightly metrics log (`pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv`... accuracy was 0.90/0.92/0.91 on 2026-06-14/15/16, then drops to exactly `0.00` starting 2026-06-17 — the same date as commit `53551e8`

report_1.evidence: The investigation used `pipeline/score_one.py`, which — as shown in `score_one.py:11-12` — only calls `predict_category(normalize_simple(text))` and prints the prediction. It never reads `config/scoring_config.yaml`, never loads a gold label, and never computes a hit/miss comparison. So it is structurally incapable of exercising the buggy code path... The correct way to reproduce it: run the actual nightly entry point, `pipeline/score_batch.score()`

report_2.evidence: `pipeline/score_one.py` (`score_single`) never scores anything against a gold label — it only calls `predict_category(normalize_simple(text))` and prints the raw prediction. It contains no gold-field lookup, no config read, and no comparison logic at all... The correct way to reproduce the drop is to run the actual batch scorer, `pipeline/score_batch.py`

report_1.evidence: Swapping the "model" therefore cannot change the outcome, since the bug is in the label-field lookup (`config["expected_label_field"]`), which is independent of any model. Acting on this plan would burn effort/rollout risk on an unrelated system while leaving the actual defect (and the 0.0 accuracy) fully in place.

report_2.evidence: "Switching the nightly runner back to the previous model" is a no-op at best (there's no model-selection code path to switch) and at worst would burn time on a rollback that leaves the actual bug — the `expected_label_field` typo/staleness in the config — completely unaddressed, so the nightly accuracy would remain at 0.00 regardless.
